name: Castillo de Cornel, Ermita de la Vigen de la Peña y Toro de Osborne — gravel circular from Alfajarín
name_original: Castillo de Cornel, Ermita de la Vigen de la Peña y Toro de Osborne — gravel circular desde Alfajarín
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alfajarín are set within the diverse landscapes of the Ebro Valley in Aragon, Spain. The region features extensive networks of back roads and trails, offering varied scenery for cyclists. The terrain primarily consists of plains, providing a contrast to more rugged mountain areas. This area provides access to a range of cycling experiences, from gravel routes to paths passing by notable local landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Alfajarín The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de Cornel, Ermita de la Vigen de la Peña y Toro de Osborne — gravel circular from Alfajarín, an 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route is a gravel circular passing by historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Galachos de La Alfranca – Bridge over the Gállego River loop from Nuez de Ebro, a moderate 30.6 miles (49.2 km) path. This loop offers views of the Ebro River plains and natural surroundings. Local touring cyclists also love the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock – Alfajarín Castle loop from Alfajarín, a 15.4 miles (24.7 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Alfajarín is defined by the plains of the Ebro Valley, an extensive network of back roads, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Alfajarín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alfajarín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alfajarín?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in the Alfajarín area, catering to various skill levels. This includes 4 easy routes, 23 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alfajarín?
A: The routes primarily traverse the diverse plains of the Ebro Valley, offering varied scenery that contrasts with more rugged mountain areas found elsewhere in Aragon. You'll find extensive networks of back roads and trails, providing a mix of natural surroundings and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes near Alfajarín?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993111370/castillo-de-cornel-ermita-de-la-vigen-de-la-pena-y-toro-de-osborne-gravel-circular-from-alfajarin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Cornel, Ermita de la Vigen de la Peña y Toro de Osborne — gravel circular from Alfajarín&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 29.8 km gravel route passing by historical landmarks. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11779386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock – Alfajarín Castle loop from Alfajarín&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.7 km trail through the local landscape.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alfajarín?
A: The touring cycling routes around Alfajarín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the blend of natural and historical sights.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alfajarín?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the area offers 4 easy routes that could be suitable for families. These routes generally feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a more relaxed cycling experience through the Ebro Valley plains.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling around Alfajarín?
A: You can incorporate several historical and cultural points of interest into your cycling tours. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfajarín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Sabina&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cabezo de la Horca&lt;/a&gt;. These sites add an enriching dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any natural attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural attractions. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2855635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de La Alfranca Park&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sabina de Villamayor&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de El Burgo de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;. These natural monuments provide scenic views and a chance to experience the Ebro Valley's ecology.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alfajarín?
A: Alfajarín benefits from a generally favorable climate for cycling. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring, avoiding the peak heat of summer. However, the Ebro Valley's diverse landscapes can be enjoyed throughout much of the year.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes available. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993111370/castillo-de-cornel-ermita-de-la-vigen-de-la-pena-y-toro-de-osborne-gravel-circular-from-alfajarin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Cornel, Ermita de la Vigen de la Peña y Toro de Osborne — gravel circular from Alfajarín&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 29.8 km with significant elevation gain, including segments that may require pushing a bike.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Ebro River?
A: Yes, several routes provide views of the Ebro River and its surrounding plains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31108390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galachos de La Alfranca – Bridge over the Gállego River loop from Nuez de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 49.2 km loop that specifically offers views of the Ebro River plains and natural surroundings, showcasing the region's unique riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through multiple towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect various local settlements, allowing you to experience more of the region. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuez de Ebro – Villafranca de Ebro loop from Alfajarín&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33 km route that takes you through different villages, offering a glimpse into local life and scenery.
tips: Located in the arid desert of Los Monegros, we find this small pool of water where you can observe birds of different species.
Full of gravel roads to fully enjoy the views of the Monegros desert, it is advisable to get lost there to enjoy the landscape

How many times have we seen it from the road and thought about going up? Well, do not hesitate any longer and come to the foot of the Toro de Osborne from where we will have magnificent views of the entire Ebro valley and Zaragoza. Nearby we can visit the Castle of Cornel and the Hermitage of the Virgen de la Peña.

From the top of a hill, the Hermitage of the Virgen de la Peña and the Castle of Cornel, dominate the town of Alfajarín, the hermitage is a construction framed in the late Gothic style of the 16th century and the castle of the 11th century, with the mission to monitor the banks of the Ebro and protect Zaragoza from the east. Both buildings located in an environment with magnificent views of the valley to the south and the foothills of the Monegros to the north. It is definitely worth a visit. They share a parking lot with the Alfajarín castle and not far away we can reach the popular Osborne bull

waypoints.name: Cornel Castle and Hermitage of the Virgen de la Peña
